novel_features.name: Fleet composition report
novel_features.description: One command turns an owner name into a full fleet profile: aircraft count, model mix, jet/turboprop/piston split, average seats and year built.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining the owner's registrations to the FAA aircraft-model reference table, which only coexist in the local synced database.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when asked what aircraft an operator or person owns — it answers in aggregate instead of forcing a page-through of raw tail lists.

novel_features.name: Batch Mode S hex resolution
novel_features.description: Resolve any number of ADS-B Mode S hex codes (args or stdin) to N-numbers, aircraft types, and owners — offline.
novel_features.rationale: Joins each hex against the locally indexed FAA registry with a pure-math fallback for unassigned codes; no rate-limited web calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to identify aircraft from ADS-B receiver logs or flight-tracker hex codes in bulk, instantly and without network access.

novel_features.name: Aircraft ownership history
novel_features.description: Chronological owner timeline for a tail number, stitching current registration with every deregistration record.
novel_features.rationale: The FAA website shows only the current owner; the timeline requires joining the active registry with the 383K-row deregistration file locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pre-purchase and title research: see who held an aircraft, when registrations were cancelled, and export history in one answer.

novel_features.name: Expiring registrations
novel_features.description: List registrations expiring within a window, filtered by owner or state, sorted soonest-first.
novel_features.rationale: US registrations expire on a 7-year cycle; no FAA endpoint exposes expiration queries — only the synced local database can answer them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catch a lapsing registration (a closing risk and airworthiness problem) before the FAA letter arrives.

novel_features.name: Model-class fleet breakdown
novel_features.description: For any make/model, break down every registered example by registrant type (corporate, individual, LLC, co-owned) and state.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates the full registry by model code — a query the one-at-a-time FAA inquiry pages cannot express.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Market research on a model class: how many exist, who owns them, and where they are based.

novel_features.name: Offline N-number availability
novel_features.description: Check whether an N-number is assigned, reserved, or free — computed locally, with the reason.
novel_features.rationale: The FAA's own availability page rejects scripted requests; the local MASTER, RESERVED, and DEREG tables answer it batch-scale with status decoding.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Vanity tail-number shopping and registration planning without fighting the website's form validation.

narrative.headline: Every FAA aircraft lookup the registry website offers, plus a daily-synced offline copy of the entire US registry that unlocks fleet reports, hex decoding, ownership history, and expiration alerts no other tool has.
narrative.value_prop: The FAA registry is browser-only and un-scriptable; existing wrappers parse stale CSVs or do one-off hex math. This CLI does both live and local: every inquiry page as a typed-JSON command, and the full 315K-aircraft registry (plus deregistered and reserved data) in SQLite for fleet report, hex resolve, aircraft history, expiring, and instant offline lookups.

quickstart.comment: Flagship: full registration record for a tail number, live from registry.faa.gov
Download the FAA's daily Releasable Aircraft Database into local SQLite (~73 MB, one-time then daily refresh)
Aggregate an owner's entire fleet: models, engine classes, seats, years
Turn ADS-B Mode S hex codes into tail numbers and owners, offline (also reads stdin, one per line)
Ownership timeline including deregistration records the website hides
Registrations lapsing in the next year — renewals cluster at month-ends, so use a wide window

troubleshoots.fix: The FAA blocks non-browser user agents; the CLI sends a Chrome User-Agent automatically — if you overrode headers, remove the override. If it persists, Akamai may be rate-limiting: wait a minute and retry.

troubleshoots.symptom: fleet report / hex resolve / expiring return 'no local database'
troubleshoots.fix: Run `faa-registry-pp-cli sync` first — offline commands need the daily FAA database snapshot.

troubleshoots.symptom: aircraft lookup returns a record but offline commands disagree
troubleshoots.fix: The bulk database refreshes nightly (~11:30pm Central) while the live site updates each federal working day at midnight; run `sync` to re-align.

troubleshoots.symptom: Owner search returns too many or zero rows
troubleshoots.fix: The FAA matches from the start of the name in ALL CAPS; try the exact registered prefix (e.g. 'NETJETS SALES') or use offline `search` for fuzzy FTS matching.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI whenever a task involves US aircraft identity: resolving a tail number or Mode S hex to an owner and aircraft type, checking registration status or expiration, researching who owns a fleet or a model class, or verifying N-number availability. It is authoritative (FAA source data), free, and works offline after one sync. It does not track live flights — pair it with a flight-tracking tool for positions.

recipes.title: Identify a plane you flew on
recipes.explanation: Full registration record narrowed to the fields that answer 'whose plane is this?' — including any co-owner names.

recipes.title: Profile an operator's fleet
recipes.explanation: Counts, model mix, and age profile for every aircraft registered to the owner, computed from the local registry.

recipes.title: Bulk-resolve ADS-B hex captures
recipes.explanation: Each Mode S hex becomes tail number + model + owner, joined offline against the FAA database. Pipe a file of codes to stdin for bulk runs.

recipes.title: Pre-purchase due diligence
recipes.explanation: Chronological ownership timeline with deregistration and cancel dates the FAA website doesn't show.

recipes.title: Find lapsing registrations
recipes.explanation: Registrations expiring in the next 60 days, soonest first.
